sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is used for advanced-stage disease, inadequate response to skin-directed therapy, or aggressive subtypes. Treatment is often continuous or maintenance to control disease, as durable remissions off treatment are uncommon. Selection depends on disease subtype, stage, prior therapies, comorbidities, and patient preferences. Single agents are preferred over combination chemotherapy due to lower cumulative toxicity.

cards.name: Brentuximab vedotin
cards.drugs: brentuximab vedotin 1.8 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks
cards.detail: Setting: MF (≥IB), PC-ALCL. Cycle days: 21-day cycle for up to 16 cycles. Key toxicities: Peripheral neuropathy (69%), neutropenia, fatigue.

cards.name: Mogamulizumab
cards.drugs: mogamulizumab 1 mg/kg IV weekly for first cycle, then days 1 and 15
cards.detail: Setting: MF (≥IB), SS. Cycle days: 28-day cycle. Key toxicities: Infusion-related reactions, skin eruptions, diarrhea.

cards.name: Bexarotene
cards.detail: Setting: MF/SS. Cycle days: Continuous. Key toxicities: Hypertriglyceridemia, hypercholesterolemia, hypothyroidism.

cards.name: Vorinostat
cards.detail: Setting: MF/SS. Cycle days: Continuous. Key toxicities: Fatigue, diarrhea, nausea, thrombocytopenia.

cards.name: Romidepsin
cards.detail: Setting: MF/SS. Cycle days: 28-day cycle. Key toxicities: QTc prolongation, nausea, fatigue.

timeline.title: MF/SS - Early stage (IB-IIA) with inadequate response to skin-directed therapy
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Bexarotene, brentuximab vedotin, interferon alfa, methotrexate, mogamulizumab, romidepsin, vorinostat. Rationale: Lower toxicity profiles; can be combined with skin-directed therapy. Alternatives: Acitretin, denileukin diftitox-cxdl, ECP, interferon gamma-1b, isotretinoin, alemtuzumab, gemcitabine, liposomal doxorubicin, pembrolizumab, pralatrexate. Biomarker requirements: CD30 expression for brentuximab vedotin; CCR4 expression not required for mogamulizumab.

timeline.title: MF/SS - Advanced stage (≥IIB) or refractory to skin-directed therapy
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Brentuximab vedotin, gemcitabine, liposomal doxorubicin, pralatrexate, romidepsin, or combination with skin-directed therapy. Rationale: Higher efficacy for advanced disease; can be combined with skin-directed therapy to maximize response. Alternatives: Bexarotene, brentuximab vedotin, denileukin diftitox-cxdl, interferon alfa, methotrexate, mogamulizumab, pembrolizumab, multiagent chemotherapy regimens (for refractory disease). Biomarker requirements: CD30 for brentuximab vedotin; HTLV-1 testing (rapid progression reported with pembrolizumab in HTLV+ patients).

timeline.title: MF with Large Cell Transformation (LCT)
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Systemic therapy (brentuximab vedotin, gemcitabine, liposomal doxorubicin, pralatrexate, romidepsin) ± RT for local control. Rationale: LCT often indicates more aggressive disease requiring systemic therapy. Alternatives: Pembrolizumab, multiagent chemotherapy regimens (for refractory disease). Biomarker requirements: CD30 expression may guide use of brentuximab vedotin.

timeline.title: Sézary Syndrome
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Mogamulizumab (high burden), romidepsin (high burden), bexarotene, ECP, interferon alfa, methotrexate, vorinostat. Rationale: Higher efficacy in blood compartment with mogamulizumab; ECP appropriate for blood involvement. Alternatives: Alemtuzumab, brentuximab vedotin, gemcitabine, liposomal doxorubicin, pembrolizumab, pralatrexate. Biomarker requirements: CCR4 expression not required for mogamulizumab.

timeline.title: PCDLBCL, leg type
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Chemoimmunotherapy (R-CHOP) ± ISRT. Rationale: Aggressive subtype requiring systemic treatment. Alternatives: Other anthracycline-based regimens. Biomarker requirements: MYD88 and CD79B mutations (associated with inferior prognosis).

timeline.title: PC-ALCL - Multifocal lesions
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Brentuximab vedotin. Rationale: Superior efficacy in ALCANZA trial. Alternatives: Methotrexate (≤50 mg weekly), systemic retinoids (bexarotene), pralatrexate, interferon. Biomarker requirements: CD30 expression (though responses seen across all levels).

timeline.title: SPTCL - With HLH or high tumor burden
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Etoposide-based combination chemotherapy (to control HLH) followed by disease-specific therapy (cyclosporine, pralatrexate, romidepsin). Rationale: Control HLH first, then target lymphoma. Alternatives: Single agents with/without prednisone. Biomarker requirements: HAVCR2 germline mutations (may confer resistance to CHOP).
